source: Claude session audit (2026-05-29) — privacy loophole sweep at Ash's request
title: PRIVACY INCIDENT — public GitHub repo leaks architecture/flowcharts, archived code, god-mode design, and vault data
what_failed: github.com/Ashar117/pi is PUBLIC ('visibility: public') and has been pushed (origin/main @ 1eb1af3). 131 files are publicly exposed. This violates all three stated privacy requirements: (1) Pi DNA private, (2) all flowcharts/architecture private, (3) Obsidian vault data private.

architecture_flowcharts: PI.md (full architecture, memory tiers, mode layer, file map)
god_mode_design: tickets T-082, T-086, T-095 (god collapse / sprint isolation / cross-device)

protected_good: No live code (agent/, tools/, pi_agent.py, core/, prompts/, scripts/, memory/) — gitignored, never pushed
No god_memory.db / god_consciousness / logs/ / data/ / .env / app/config.py
No real secrets in .env.example / SUPABASE_SETUP.sql (placeholders only)
hard_truth: Git history is permanent and this content has been PUBLIC. Making the repo private or deleting files now REDUCES future exposure but cannot guarantee removal from the world: GitHub caches, existing forks/clones, search-engine + Archive.org indexing, and AI training crawlers may already hold copies. Treat the already-public content as potentially compromised.
migration_plan: 1. DONE 2026-05-29: github.com/Ashar117/pi set to PRIVATE via GitHub API (PATCH private=true, HTTP 200; unauthenticated GET now 404) using Ash's stored Git Credential Manager credential. New public access is stopped. NOTE: ~5 weeks of prior public exposure (repo created 2026-04-24) is NOT retractable — assume scraped/cached/indexed.
2. Do NOT push the local fix/conversation-coherence commit (f71f20e) — its tickets are detailed flowcharts. Keep it private-only.

3. Decide repo model: since DNA + flowcharts + vault are ALL private, effectively nothing in this repo is 'product'. Make this repo the PRIVATE source of truth (track everything incl. code behind a private remote). 'Product available' = the running agent/service, not a public repo. Any public face later = a fresh, tiny, recipe-free marketing repo.

4. Fix structural cause: 'archive to docs/_archive/ instead of delete' moves private code into PUBLIC docs/. Gitignore docs/_archive/ (or whole-repo-private moots it). privacy_publish_guard must FAIL on any docs/**/*.py and on tracked vault/, CHECKPOINTS/, docs/adr/.
5. Harden .gitignore (only relevant if any public repo remains): vault/, CHECKPOINTS/, docs/adr/, docs/**/*.py, PI.md, ABOUT.md architecture, ADRs.

6. Optional after going private: git filter-repo to purge the most sensitive paths from history (god ADR/tickets, archived .py) — meaningful for limiting collaborator/clone access, not for undoing public exposure.
analysis: PERSPECTIVE: This is the most serious finding of the entire session and it outranks every coherence ticket. The project's #1 stated value is 'sell the product, not the recipe' — and the recipe (architecture, flowcharts, ADRs, archived code, god-mode design, engineering tickets) has been sitting in a PUBLIC repo. The good news: the live code and god source were correctly protected, and no secrets leaked. The bad news: the entire 'how it works' layer is exactly what you wanted private, and it's been public and likely scraped. Two lessons: (a) privacy-by-gitignore only works if the repo's default is private — a public repo with an allowlist mindset is one archive-to-docs/ away from leaking; flip it to private-by-default. (b) The privacy_publish_guard checks for tracked IMPL files and secrets but NOT for architecture docs / flowcharts / vault being public — it passed Sections 1-2 while the recipe was wide open. The guard's threat model was too narrow. Make the repo private first, then redesign the public/private split from a private-by-default baseline.
